our_open_deliverables.text: Share the demo agenda after an internal alignment call, keeping eProcurement at the far end
Create high-level 3DX/PLM wireframes and internally assess how much PLM development Zycus will commit to

buyer_dependent_items.text: Send a written statement clarifying which RFP items (3DX/PLM and VMI) are must-have vs nice-to-have for scoring
Share the latest SME list (~35 people) and confirmed demo scheduling availability

competitive_position.summary: Zycus is the front-runner (vendor of choice, Katie Booth 24 Jun) on a three-vendor shortlist. Ranked by CURRENT threat: Oracle is the strongest live threat, not because the business wants it but because IT (CIO Bob King, lead Pete) keeps it in 'at IT's insistence' and gates integration approval, while the business is openly unhappy with it. iValua is a low, inertia-only threat ('in only because shortlisted last initiative'). Coupa is dormant, named only in the CRM field and grouped with Zycus as an indirect-only player. The deeper competitive exposure is the 3DX/PLM native-connector gap where direct-sourcing specialists (Jaggaer, Ivalua) are stronger, but the buyer has de-scoped direct materials. Win path: neutralise IT's Oracle lean via the integration deep-dive, lead with vendor-led implementation and Merlin AI, and get the value case in front of Single/Boss before finalist scoring.

competitors.name: Oracle
competitors.quote: Oracle at IT's insistence. IT is a wild card, but no one is happy with Oracle as a partner.
competitors.how_we_win: Vendor-led implementation Oracle won't offer, plus proactively closing the IT integration deep-dive to remove the only reason Oracle survives.

competitors.name: iValua
competitors.quote: iValua in only because they were shortlisted last initiative
competitors.how_we_win: Zycus is already VOC; keep the pace and vendor-led-implementation wedge that iValua won't match.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: the Coupa's and the Zycus of the world will not have direct connections with PLM
competitors.source: SF Competitors__c; Ronit Wadhwa call 24 Jun 2026
competitors.how_we_win: Not an active evaluation participant; Merlin agentic sourcing differentiates on indirect scope.

competitors.name: Jaggaer / Ivalua (direct-sourcing peers)
competitors.quote: the jugglers and the valuers of the world have connections because these serve those connections
competitors.how_we_win: Buyer has de-scoped direct materials; frame Phase 1 on indirect SRM strength and commit to a phased 3DX connector.

champion_strength.source: Avoma calls 8 Jun, 24 Jun, 30 Jun 2026; Next Step 24 Jun 2026; SF Contact Roles; Avoma attendance (Single/Boss absent)
champion_strength.summary: Yufeng Chen (Global Sourcing Manager) and Katie Booth (Global Indirect Sourcing Manager - IT) are strong, active champions with multi-call engagement (8, 24, 30 Jun) who confirmed Zycus is vendor of choice, de-risked the 3DX/PLM and VMI gaps as line items rather than deal breakers, and push back on Oracle and iValua on Zycus's behalf. What has developed: technical fit, honesty-built trust, VOC status and demo momentum. What is missing: neither has shown access to Economic Buyer David Single or Executive Sponsor Jim Boss, so advocacy is powerful at the manager layer but cannot yet convert to budget sign-off. Kara Weiner (VP Supply Chain) is a senior advocate for vendor-led implementation per Next Step but has not appeared on a recorded call, so her engagement is unconfirmed.
champion_strength.champion: Yufeng Chen (Global Sourcing Manager), Katie Booth (Global Indirect Sourcing Manager - IT)

sources: SF Contact Roles (David Single, Jim Boss, Kara Weiner)
meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: The economic layer is mapped in Salesforce but not engaged. David Single (Vice President Finance) is the budget owner and Jim Boss (Vice President, Global Operational Excellence) is the Executive Sponsor per Contact Roles, and Next Step (24 Jun) names the VP of Supply Chain (Kara Weiner) as wanting a vendor-led implementation. None of the three has appeared on any of the 13+ recorded buyer calls from Feb to Jun 2026; every commercial conversation has run through managers Yufeng Chen and Katie Booth. Pricing is entering the conversation (Yufeng 30 Jun) with no EB in the room, so engineering a Single/Boss value review before finalist scoring is the highest-leverage power move.

recommended_moves: Send Dan Chao the module-by-module demo agenda mapping each RFP tab to SME sessions and lock 6-17 Jul dates.
Get Yufeng Chen's written must-have vs nice-to-have call on 3DX/PLM and VMI before scoring.
Broker an executive value review for David Single and Jim Boss committing vendor-led implementation and resources.
